sources.title: Prometheus Overview
sources.scope_notes: Backs Prometheus as a pull-based metrics system with labeled time series, HTTP scraping, service discovery, local storage, recording rules, alert generation, and common ecosystem components such as Alertmanager and Grafana.

sources.title: Prometheus Configuration
sources.scope_notes: Backs Kubernetes service discovery roles, scrape configuration, relabeling-related target metadata, and the mechanics used to debug missing or mislabeled scrape targets in Kubernetes.

sources.title: OpenTelemetry Collector
sources.scope_notes: Backs the Collector as the vendor-neutral layer for receiving, processing, and exporting telemetry, including the receiver/processor/exporter pipeline model and backend-neutral architecture.

sources.title: OTLP Specification 1.10.0
sources.scope_notes: Backs OTLP as the OpenTelemetry transport protocol, including gRPC and HTTP transport details, request/response behavior, default ports, retry semantics, and signal transport expectations.

sources.title: Grafana Documentation
sources.scope_notes: Broad official docs hub for Grafana dashboards, variables, data sources, panels, Explore, correlations, and operational workflows that connect metrics, logs, and traces.

sources.title: Grafana Alerting
sources.scope_notes: Backs Grafana-managed alert rules, evaluation behavior, notification routing, contact points, grouping, silences, and other alerting concepts relevant to dashboard-adjacent operations.

sources.title: OpenSLO Specification
sources.scope_notes: Backs OpenSLO as a vendor-neutral specification for expressing SLOs, SLIs, objectives, alert policies, services, and budgeting methods in portable YAML.

sources.title: Grafana Loki: Query Loki (LogQL)
sources.scope_notes: Backs Loki’s label-based query model, log streams, compressed chunk storage, label indexing instead of full-content indexing, and LogQL basics for log exploration and metric extraction from logs.

sources.title: OpenTelemetry: Traces
sources.scope_notes: Backs core tracing concepts: traces, spans, parent-child relationships, span context, attributes, events, links, status, and how a request path is represented across services.

sources.title: OpenTelemetry: Sampling
sources.scope_notes: Backs head-vs-tail sampling concepts, cost-control rationale, and practical guidance about reducing trace volume while preserving useful signal.

sources.title: Grafana Tempo: Architecture
sources.scope_notes: Backs Tempo’s backend architecture, object-storage-based trace retention, TraceQL search, ingestion/query path, and tradeoff discussions for self-managed tracing backends.

sources.title: Parca Agent README
sources.scope_notes: Backs Parca Agent claims: always-on sampling profiler, eBPF-based stack collection, Kubernetes/systemd target discovery, zero code changes or restarts, supported languages, Linux/BTF requirements, and currently supported profile scope.

sources.title: Parca README
sources.scope_notes: Backs Parca server/product claims: continuous profiling over time, line-level analysis, pprof interoperability, optimized storage/querying, infrastructure-wide aggregation, and comparison across dimensions for regression analysis.

sources.title: Pyroscope and Profiling in Grafana
sources.scope_notes: Backs Pyroscope/Grafana workflow claims: continuous profiling in Grafana, correlation with metrics/logs/traces, traces-to-profiles navigation, and dashboard integration.

sources.title: Pyroscope Profile Types and Instrumentation
sources.scope_notes: Backs profile-type claims and caveats: CPU, memory, goroutine, mutex, block, wall, heap, lock, exceptions; also backs that eBPF auto-instrumentation primarily provides CPU profiling while other types depend on language/runtime instrumentation.
